methodology_long: Rankings are generated by a shadow roto model. Each week, all twelve teams are scored across the fourteen active categories (R, HR, RBI, SB, AVG, OBP, SLG, W, K, ERA, WHIP, K/BB, QS, SV+H) based on an estimate of each team's true strength — not by their actual head-to-head matchup result, which depends on schedule luck. Each team receives 12 points for first in a category down to 1 point for last; the Power Score is the sum (max 168, min 14), and that's the rank. True strength is estimated with a Bayesian prior (the preseason projection) blended with each completed week's real performance using exponential decay, so recent weeks count more than older ones. Rate categories (ERA, AVG, etc.) are computed from weighted components (e.g., total earned runs over total innings pitched), not from averaging the week-level rates.

rankings.blurb: Pulled away at the top. CLAUDE crushed Polar 11-3-2 in Week 8 with a monster offensive line (.313/.370/.543, 47 R, 13 HR, 44 RBI) and now ranks top-3 in nine of fourteen categories — including #1 in AVG. The Week 7 tie with Duran was a one-week blip; Week 8 made up for it. The team's only soft cells remain SV+H (8th) and QS (8th), neither of which is fixable without a roster move. At 134 power, this is the highest single-team score the model has produced this season.

Up one to #2. Beat Duran 9-3-4 in Week 8 with a clean pitching line (3.67 ERA, 6 W, 5 QS in 76 IP) and a strong offense (44 R, 10 HR). Cumulative ranks now first in W and QS, second in WHIP and OBP, third in ERA and K/BB. Floor remains the highest pitching profile in the league. The offensive weaknesses haven't moved — still bottom-tier in SB, RBI, and HR — but it doesn't matter when the staff is this efficient.

Up two despite continued pitching disaster. Beat Balls 8-6-2 in Week 8. Still leading the league in R, HR, RBI, OBP, and SV+H — five #1 category ranks, no other team has more than two. The pitching IP totals have now hit 28.0 (W7) and 23.0 (W8) — last in the league by huge margins, and dead-last in W, K, and QS cumulatively. The model still rewards the elite offense enough to put UBC at #3, but the structural imbalance is now permanent until a trade happens.

Holds at #4. Lost to Camp 4-10-2 in Week 8 in the most surprising result of the week — the offense managed only 36 R despite an 11-HR effort. Cumulative ranks still leads the league in SLG, 2nd in AVG, and 5th in ERA. Underlying matchup record (2-4-2) is well below the power score; the schedule is doing this team no favors. The K/BB weakness remains the structural issue (12th in the league).

Drops three after a Week 8 thud — 1 W, 5.12 ERA in 65 IP, .222 AVG. Beat Y&Y 7-5-4 anyway because the bats woke up enough (8 HR, 29 RBI). Still has the best actual matchup record in the league at 6-1-1, and ranks 2nd in HR and 4th in K cumulatively. The model has been more skeptical than Yahoo's W-L record for weeks — this is the rank converging downward to where the underlying stats suggest. The pitching has cooled noticeably from the Weeks 5-6 peak.

Up one. Beat ShawSwank 8-6-2 in Week 8 (3.50 ERA, 6 W, 5 QS, 0.97 WHIP) — vintage Spoons pitching. The Week 7 explosion (78 K, 2.30 ERA, 0.84 WHIP, 6.00 K/BB) is a top-three single-week pitching line of the year. Now leads the league in WHIP, K, K/BB, and ranks 2nd in QS. The offense remains the structural problem — 11th in AVG, 11th in R, 10th in RBI. Matchup record (5-1-2) is tied for second-best.

Down one. Lost to Caminero 5-7-4 in Week 8 with a soft .220 AVG and 8 HR. Still ranks 2nd in R and top-5 in HR, RBI, AVG, OBP, SLG — the offense is the second-best in the league. The pitching weakness is now permanent (last or near-last in QS, ERA, WHIP, K all season). Matchup record (4-3-1) is honest about the team's level. Without a pitching upgrade, the ceiling is this rank.

Up two on the back of two strong pitching weeks. Week 7: 7 W, 7 QS, 2.78 ERA, 1.06 WHIP. Week 8: 5 W, 8 QS, 2.38 ERA, 0.96 WHIP. Lost both matchups anyway (to Y&Y in W7 tie, UBC in W8) because the offense has gone cold (.258, .245 AVG those weeks). Cumulative ranks now 2nd in W, 3rd in QS, 4th in ERA and WHIP. The bullpen continues to be the worst in the league (11th in SV+H, 1 SV+H in W8) but the SP volume is finally paying off.

Down one. Beat Camp 10-3-2 in Week 7 then lost to Spoons 6-8-2 in Week 8. The pitching peripherals are still strong — 78 K and 4.59 K/BB in W8 — but the offense remains an anchor: bottom-three in HR, RBI, and SLG all season. The 5-3 matchup record is well ahead of the model's #9 rank, which says either Yahoo is giving ShawSwank too much credit or the underlying offensive numbers are about to bite the matchup record back.

rankings.team_name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints
rankings.blurb: Up one. Crushed PO' BOYD 10-4-2 in Week 8 — 9 W, 92 K, .259/.375/.451, 6 QS in 80.1 IP. That's the biggest single-week pitching volume any Camp lineup has produced this year. Lost to ShawSwank 3-10-2 in Week 7. The cumulative profile still has Camp at 12th in SV+H and 11th in WHIP and ERA — the bullpen is just thin. But the underlying offensive profile (top-7 in R, HR, RBI) and the K/BB profile (5th) say the team is better than the matchup record suggests.

Drops two. Lost to CLAUDE 3-11-2 in Week 8 in the worst category result of the entire season. The Week 7 win against Caminero on paper (5-9 in matchup score — so actually a loss to Caminero) and an outright loss to Duran in W8 are part of a now seven-game skid. The pitching profile has finally cracked too (5.74 ERA in W7, 3.95 in W8 but only 43.1 IP). Now last in the power rankings; the trajectory has flipped from "finally trending up" two weeks ago to "freefall."

Stays last. Tied CLAUDE 7-7-2 in Week 7 — the high-water mark of the season for any matchup involving Duran — then lost to Burns 3-9-4 in Week 8. Now leads the league in SB at 15 in Week 7 alone, but the offensive slash line is dead-last in AVG, OBP, AND SLG cumulatively. Pitching is bottom-three in WHIP, K/BB, and OBP. The roster construction problem hasn't moved; two narrow categories of strength against eleven categories of weakness.
